title: ipv6_addr.c:91 goto cleanup without setting ret on success path
description:
In lichen_pubkey_to_iid(), at line 127 there's a 'return ret;' that follows the cleanup label. Looking at the success path:

1. lichen_sha256() succeeds (line 88-92) - ret is set to 0 on success
2. memcpy and U/L bit clearing (lines 105-121) - no ret modification
3. Falls through to cleanup (line 123)
4. secure_zero() the hash (line 125)
5. return ret (line 126)

Actually ret is initialized to 0 (via the int ret declaration and the success return from lichen_sha256). If sha256 returns 0, ret stays 0 through cleanup.

Wait, looking at line 88-92:
```c
ret = lichen_sha256(pubkey, LICHEN_ED25519_PUBKEY_LEN, hash);
if (ret != 0) {
    LOG_ERR(...);
    goto cleanup;
}
```

If sha256 succeeds, ret == 0. Then lines 105-121 execute, then cleanup runs, and ret (still 0) is returned. This is correct.

NO BUG.
